NSKFDC is in operation since October, 1997, as an Apex Corporation for the all round socio-economic upliftment of the Safai Karamcharis, Scavengers and their dependants throughout India,through various loan and non-loan based schemes. Apart from operating various loan and non-loan based schemes for the upliftment of the target group. pages.title: Manual Scavengers | National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation
pages.text: Manual Scavengers | National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation NSKFDC × SRMS DASHBOARD Skip to Main Content Screen Reader Access English हिन्दी Facebook Twitter Youtube Donate us Manual Scavengers A person engaged or employed by an individual or a local authority or an agency or a contractor, for manually cleaning, carrying, disposing of, or otherwise handling in any manner, human excreta in an insanitary latrine or in an open drain or pit into which the human excreta from the insanitary latrines is disposed of, or on railway track or in such other spaces or premises, as the Central Government or a State Government may notify, before the excreta fully decomposes in such a manner as may be prescribed, and the expression ‘’Manual Scavenging” shall be construed accordingly. a) “engaged or employed’’ means being engaged or employed on a regular or contract basis; b) a person engaged or employed to clean excreta with the help of such devices and using such protective gear, as the Central Government may notify in this behalf, shall not be deemed to be a ‘manual scavenger’. pages.title: Eligibility Criteria | National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation
pages.text: Eligibility Criteria | National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation NSKFDC × SRMS DASHBOARD Skip to Main Content Screen Reader Access English हिन्दी Facebook Twitter Youtube Donate us Eligibility Criteria Safai Karamcharis (including Wastepickers), identified Manual Scavengers and their dependents. Registered co-operative societies of Safai Karamcharis. Legally constituted association/firm promoted by the target group, and All those who produce a certificate from local Revenue Officer/local Municipal Officer/Cantonment Executive Officer/Railway Officer, Head of the Govt. Departments (i.e. Schools, Colleges, Forest, Health, Education, Animal Husbandry) having rank not less than Gazetted Officer, Elected Members of Municipal Body & Pradhan of Gram Panchayats, Regional Mangers of Regional Rural Banks(RRBs)/Nationalised Banks. However, under the MS Act, 2013, a person identified as Manual Scavenger in a survey, need not to provide any certificate once his/her name appeared in the final list of Manual Scavengers prepared by State Governments/Union Territory Administration. No income limit is fixed for availing financial assistance. However, other things being equal, NSKFDC accords priority to economic development and rehabilitation of – Scavengers and amongst scavengers those scavengers whose income is below double the poverty line; Women from among the target group and Disabled persons among the target group. Financial viability, income generating capacity of the project etc. are considered while evaluating the projects for financing. pages.title: Non Loan Based Schemes | National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation
pages.text: Non Loan Based Schemes | National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation NSKFDC × SRMS DASHBOARD Skip to Main Content Screen Reader Access English हिन्दी Facebook Twitter Youtube Donate us Non Loan Based Schemes In order to support the target group, the Corporation extends financial support under its various non-credit based schemes as follows: - AWARENESS CAMPS Workshops Job Fair Marketing Linkages/Melas/Exhibitions/Trade Fairs: NSKFDC participates in National / State Level Exhibitions & Fairs and provides stalls free of cost to the beneficiaries for exhibiting and selling their products. Participation in these exhibitions provides the opportunities to the beneficiaries for not only in selling their products but also interacting with the customers and knowing the customer's needs/requirements for development of new products. The rent for providing the stalls in the exhibitions at prominent places like Dilli Haat (INA, New Delhi), India International Trade Fair (IITF) (Pragti Maidan, New Delhi), Suraj Kund Mela (Faridabad, Haryana) are being shared by the Ministry and the Apex Corporations/Institutes as per the guidelines issued by the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment from time to time. In order to meet out the expenditure of beneficiaries for participating in the exhibitions/fairs, NSKFDC has been reimbursing to beneficiaries actual to & fro train/bus fare, lodging charges @ Rs.300/-, Food charges @ Rs.150/- and local conveyance expenses @ Rs.150/- per day/per beneficiary. pages.title: General Term Loan (TL) | National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation
pages.text: General Term Loan (TL) | National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation NSKFDC × SRMS DASHBOARD Skip to Main Content Screen Reader Access English हिन्दी Facebook Twitter Youtube Donate us General Term Loan (TL) | Maximum Limit | Interest Chargeable from | | | SCA | Beneficiary | | Upto Rs. 15.00 Lac | 4.5% p.a. | 9% p.a. | Repayment period: Upto 10 years after implementation period of 4 months and moratorium of 6 months and from the date of disbursement of loan depending on the viability/profitability and repaying capacity of the unit. Under this scheme, term loans are extended through State Channelising Agencies (SCA), Regional Rural Banks(RRBs) and Nationalised Banks to the target group. Under this scheme assistance is provided for any viable income generating schemes including sanitation related activities with a maximum project cost of Rs.15.00 lac. Promoter’s contribution is not insisted upon for projects costing upto Rs.2.00 lac. For projects costing more than Rs.2.00 lac , minimum promoter’s contribution insisted by NSKFDC from the beneficiaries is 5% and 5%is to be provided by SCA. General Term loan can be provided up to maximum of 90% of the unit cost and the remaining 10% share is to be provided by the State Channelising Agencies in the form of loan, subsidy or promoter’s contribution, or from any other available sources of funds. pages.text: Education Loan (EL) | National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation NSKFDC × SRMS DASHBOARD Skip to Main Content Screen Reader Access English हिन्दी Facebook Twitter Youtube Donate us Education Loan (EL) Education Loan is being extended to the students from the community of Safai Karamcharis\Manual Scavengers & their dependents for pursuing Professional or Technical Education of Graduation or higher levels in Medical, Engineering, Management, Law, IT/Computers. Graduation courses in all disciplines (i.e. B.A., B.Sc; & B.Com etc). Sanitary Inspector & other similar job oriented courses having minimum period of one year, Diploma in Physiotherapy, Pathology, Nursing, Hotel Management & Tourism, Journalism & Mass Communication, Geriatric Care. Academic/Professional courses at Graduate & Post Graduate level like Bachelor of Education, Ph.D, Language courses, BCA, MCA etc; (from authorized/recognized Institutes/Universities). | Maximum Limit | Interest Chargeable from | | Study in India -upto Rs.10.00 LacStudy in Abroad -upto Rs.20.00Lac [The interest on Education Loan (for study in India) is reimbursable under the scheme of Ministry of Education,Government of India to the beneficiaries whose family annual income is upto 4.50 lac per annum] | 2% | 6% (0.5% p.a. rebate for women candidates for study in India) | | 3% p.a. | 7% p.a.(0.5% p.a. rebate for women candidates for study in India) | The interest on Education Loan is reimbursable under the scheme of Ministry of Human Resource Development, Govt. of India to the beneficiaries whose family income is upto Rs.4.50 lac per annum. pages.title: Manual Scavengers | National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation
pages.text: Manual Scavengers | National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation NSKFDC × SRMS DASHBOARD Skip to Main Content Screen Reader Access English हिन्दी Facebook Twitter Youtube Donate us Manual Scavengers A person engaged or employed by an individual or a local authority or an agency or a contractor, for manually cleaning, carrying, disposing of, or otherwise handling in any manner, human excreta in an insanitary latrine or in an open drain or pit into which the human excreta from the insanitary latrines is disposed of, or on railway track or in such other spaces or premises, as the Central Government or a State Government may notify, before the excreta fully decomposes in such a manner as may be prescribed, and the expression ‘’Manual Scavenging” shall be construed accordingly. a) “engaged or employed’’ means being engaged or employed on a regular or contract basis; b) a person engaged or employed to clean excreta with the help of such devices and using such protective gear, as the Central Government may notify in this behalf, shall not be deemed to be a ‘manual scavenger’. pages.title: Eligibility Criteria | National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation
pages.text: Eligibility Criteria | National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation NSKFDC × SRMS DASHBOARD Skip to Main Content Screen Reader Access English हिन्दी Facebook Twitter Youtube Donate us Eligibility Criteria Safai Karamcharis (including Wastepickers), identified Manual Scavengers and their dependents. Registered co-operative societies of Safai Karamcharis. Legally constituted association/firm promoted by the target group, and All those who produce a certificate from local Revenue Officer/local Municipal Officer/Cantonment Executive Officer/Railway Officer, Head of the Govt. Departments (i.e. Schools, Colleges, Forest, Health, Education, Animal Husbandry) having rank not less than Gazetted Officer, Elected Members of Municipal Body & Pradhan of Gram Panchayats, Regional Mangers of Regional Rural Banks(RRBs)/Nationalised Banks. However, under the MS Act, 2013, a person identified as Manual Scavenger in a survey, need not to provide any certificate once his/her name appeared in the final list of Manual Scavengers prepared by State Governments/Union Territory Administration. No income limit is fixed for availing financial assistance. However, other things being equal, NSKFDC accords priority to economic development and rehabilitation of – Scavengers and amongst scavengers those scavengers whose income is below double the poverty line; Women from among the target group and Disabled persons among the target group. Financial viability, income generating capacity of the project etc. are considered while evaluating the projects for financing. pages.title: Non Loan Based Schemes | National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation
pages.text: Non Loan Based Schemes | National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation NSKFDC × SRMS DASHBOARD Skip to Main Content Screen Reader Access English हिन्दी Facebook Twitter Youtube Donate us Non Loan Based Schemes In order to support the target group, the Corporation extends financial support under its various non-credit based schemes as follows: - AWARENESS CAMPS Workshops Job Fair Marketing Linkages/Melas/Exhibitions/Trade Fairs: NSKFDC participates in National / State Level Exhibitions & Fairs and provides stalls free of cost to the beneficiaries for exhibiting and selling their products. Participation in these exhibitions provides the opportunities to the beneficiaries for not only in selling their products but also interacting with the customers and knowing the customer's needs/requirements for development of new products. The rent for providing the stalls in the exhibitions at prominent places like Dilli Haat (INA, New Delhi), India International Trade Fair (IITF) (Pragti Maidan, New Delhi), Suraj Kund Mela (Faridabad, Haryana) are being shared by the Ministry and the Apex Corporations/Institutes as per the guidelines issued by the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment from time to time. In order to meet out the expenditure of beneficiaries for participating in the exhibitions/fairs, NSKFDC has been reimbursing to beneficiaries actual to & fro train/bus fare, lodging charges @ Rs.300/-, Food charges @ Rs.150/- and local conveyance expenses @ Rs.150/- per day/per beneficiary. pages.title: General Term Loan (TL) | National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation
pages.text: General Term Loan (TL) | National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation NSKFDC × SRMS DASHBOARD Skip to Main Content Screen Reader Access English हिन्दी Facebook Twitter Youtube Donate us General Term Loan (TL) | Maximum Limit | Interest Chargeable from | | | SCA | Beneficiary | | Upto Rs. 15.00 Lac | 4.5% p.a. | 9% p.a. | Repayment period: Upto 10 years after implementation period of 4 months and moratorium of 6 months and from the date of disbursement of loan depending on the viability/profitability and repaying capacity of the unit. Under this scheme, term loans are extended through State Channelising Agencies (SCA), Regional Rural Banks(RRBs) and Nationalised Banks to the target group. Under this scheme assistance is provided for any viable income generating schemes including sanitation related activities with a maximum project cost of Rs.15.00 lac. Promoter’s contribution is not insisted upon for projects costing upto Rs.2.00 lac. For projects costing more than Rs.2.00 lac , minimum promoter’s contribution insisted by NSKFDC from the beneficiaries is 5% and 5%is to be provided by SCA. General Term loan can be provided up to maximum of 90% of the unit cost and the remaining 10% share is to be provided by the State Channelising Agencies in the form of loan, subsidy or promoter’s contribution, or from any other available sources of funds. analysis.overview: National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation (NSKFDC) is a wholly owned Government of India undertaking under the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, established on 24th January 1997 and operational since October 1997. It serves as an apex corporation for the socio-economic upliftment of Safai Karamcharis, Scavengers, and their dependents throughout India through various loan and non-loan based schemes. NSKFDC has also been designated as the nodal agency for implementing the Central Sector National Action for Mechanized Sanitation Ecosystem (NAMASTE) scheme under the same ministry.
target_beneficiaries: dependents of Safai Karamcharis and Scavengers
registered co-operative societies of Safai Karamcharis
legally constituted associations/firms promoted by the target group
objectives: Socio-economic upliftment of Safai Karamcharis, Scavengers, and their dependents
Implementation of loan and non-loan based schemes for income generation
Rehabilitation of manual scavengers through self-employment schemes
Skill development training for target group members aged 18 to 45 years
Implementation of the NAMASTE scheme for mechanized sanitation ecosystem
Providing financial assistance without income limits, prioritizing those below double the poverty line
Supporting women and disabled persons from the target group
Promoting viability and income-generating capacity in project financing
analysis.eligibility: Safai Karamcharis (including Wastepickers), identified Manual Scavengers and their dependents. Registered co-operative societies of Safai Karamcharis. Legally constituted association/firm promoted by the target group. All those who produce a certificate from local Revenue Officer/local Municipal Officer/Cantonment Executive Officer/Railway Officer, Head of Govt. Departments (Schools, Colleges, Forest, Health, Education, Animal Husbandry) having rank not less than Gazetted Officer, Elected Members of Municipal Body & Pradhan of Gram Panchayats, Regional Managers of Regional Rural Banks (RRBs)/Nationalised Banks. Under the MS Act, 2013, a person identified as Manual Scavenger in a survey need not provide any certificate once their name appears in the final list of Manual Scavengers prepared by State Governments/Union Territory Administration. No income limit is fixed for availing financial assistance. However, priority is accorded to economic development and rehabilitation of Scavengers, those with income below double the poverty line, Women from the target group, and Disabled persons among the target group. Financial viability and income-generating capacity of the project are considered while evaluating projects for financing.
analysis.benefits: Financial support through various loan schemes including General Term Loan (up to Rs.15.00 Lac), Education Loan (up to Rs.10.00 Lac for study in India, Rs.20.00 Lac for abroad), Mahila Samridhi Yojana (up to Rs.1.00 Lac), Mahila Adhikarita Yojana (up to Rs.2.00 Lac), Micro Credit Finance (up to Rs.1.00 Lac), Scheme for 'Pay and use' community toilets (up to Rs.25.00 Lac), Sanitary Marts Scheme (up to Rs.15.00 Lac), Green Business Scheme (up to Rs.15.00 Lac), and Swachhta Udyami Yojana (up to Rs.50.00 Lac for SHG/JRG/Cooperative). Non-loan based benefits include awareness camps, workshops, job fairs, marketing linkages/melas/exhibitions/trade fairs with free stalls, reimbursement of actual to & fro train/bus fare, lodging charges @ Rs.300/-, food charges @ Rs.150/-, and local conveyance expenses @ Rs.150/- per day/per beneficiary for exhibition participation. Interest rates range from 2% to 9% p.a. depending on the scheme and beneficiary category, with rebates for women and timely repayment. Repayment periods vary from 3 to 10 years with moratorium periods. Education loan interest is reimbursable under Ministry of Education scheme for beneficiaries with family annual income up to Rs.4.50 lac.
